steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both mountain ranges in the notes share the 'sky islands' classification — isolated ranges whose environments differ from surrounding lowlands. The question asks for a sentence that emphasizes what the two ranges share, not how they differ.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "There are dozens of different mountain ranges located in the southwestern US and northwestern Mexico.": broadens to mountain ranges in general rather than focusing on these two specific ranges and what they share. ✗
  • "The Archipelago-Madrean Mountains and the Big Hatchet mountains are both inland mountain ranges whose surroundings are different from those of the surrounding mountains.": makes the main clause about the shared classification — both are isolated mountain ranges whose environments differ from their surroundings — which is exactly the unity the notes establish. ✓
  • "The Archipelago-Madrean Mountains cover an area of 1,038 mi², while the Big Hatchet Mountains cover an area of 65 mi².": foregrounds the contrast in area (1,038 vs. 65 mi²) rather than the shared category. ✗
  • "Even though they are both sky inlands, the Archipelago-Madrean Mountains and the Big Hatchet Mountains are located in different countries.": acknowledges the shared label but then makes the main point a difference in location, undermining the unity the question asks for. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
